Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly csSphComp1.1 of the haploid compact bogmoss, Sphagnum compactum. The sample was provided by Olena Meleshko & Kristian Hassel, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). csSphComp1.1 is based on 58x PacBio HiFi data and 120x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm, generating a primary assembly. The assembly was purged for haplotigs with purge_dups, before scaffolding with YaHS. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Falconiformes; Falconidae; Falco
Assembly: The assembly bFalTin1.hap1.1 is based on 66x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. The microchromosomes were identified through two stage curation of scaffolds smaller than 20 Mbp, combined with homology-based identification using known microchromosome genes via Microfinder. The Z and W chromosomes were identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (119,327 - 120,906 Kbp) are unknown. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 4 (98,667 -113,013 bp).Chromosome 6 shows heterozygous rearrangements. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (121,554 - 122,506 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W (7,828 - 13,104 & 31,399 - 47,207 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome Z (75,039 - 76,834 & 78,456 - 80,121 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly csSphRube1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome 21 and 22 are microchromosomes. Chromosome 20 is about 1/4th of the size of an autosome, which is similar to what Healey, A.L., et. al (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41477-022-01333-5) observed for potential U/V sex chromosomes in other Sphagnums.

Assembly: The assembly qqTroExca7.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/) as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas pilot project~(https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/pilot-project). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation. Sample is the homogametic sex but no X chromosome could be identified.

Assembly: The assembly ilSteVero1.hap1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). We observed a large Z chromosome that looks to have come from the fusion of many autosomes, around 8, based on the comparison with the karyotype of close species. One potential B chromosome was detected, which is in HAP1 file. We believe it is a B chromosomes because: i) after BUSCO alignment to a close related species, all Stenoptilia veronicae autosomes match one of the reference chromosomes, looking like that most of the content present in the B chromosome may be new to the genome; ii) all BUSCO hits present in the B chromosome were duplicated and iii) similarity could be observed on the HiC map between the B chromosome and some tiny regions of some of the autosomes, indicating they may have genes in common.

Assembly: The assembly daCusEpit1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Some inversions between the haplotypes were detected: 32.4 to 43 Mbp on Chromosome 2 and 36.6 to 54.56 Mbp on Chromosome 7. The region from the start to 6.2 Mbp on Chromosome 1 is highly repetitive and the order and orientation is uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly bColMon1.hap1.1 is based on 77x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (55.5 - 59 Mbp), chromosome 8 (12 - 14 Mbp) are unknown. The microchromosomes were identified through second-shot curation of scaffolds smaller than 20 Mbp, combined with homology-based identification using known microchromosome genes via MicroFinder. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.
